title: Md. Jubayer Hossain
text:
Welcome!ExperienceSkillsEducation & Training


Hello, I am Md. Jubayer Hossain. I am the founder and Executive Director of CHIRAL Bangladesh, a non-profit biomedical research organization dedicated to solving public health problems in Bangladesh. Additionally, I am also working as a health data analysis instructor at cBLAST. My professional endeavors aim to maximize the quality of life for individuals by operating at the intersection of education, technology, and health research.
I am interested in applying modern data science techniques in the context of disease diagnosis and large data analytics for public health while focusing on bridging the gap between computational and experimental laboratories through highly engaging and fruitful collaborations.


Founder & Management Lead | Center for Health Innovation, Research Action, and Learning - Bangladesh (CHIRAL Bangladesh) | Dhaka, Bangladesh | (2020 - Present)
Instructor | Center for Bioinformatics Learning Advancement and Systematic Training (cBLAST) | Dhaka, Bangladesh | (August 2023 - Present)
Program Lead | Research Internship Program | Center for Health Innovation, Research Action, and Learning - Bangladesh (CHIRAL Bangladesh) | Dhaka, Bangladesh | (January 2023 - Present)
Teaching Assistant | Public Health & Hygiene, Department of Microbiology | Jagannath University) | Dhaka, Bangladesh | (October 2022 - December 2022)
Research Intern | Health Systems and Population Health Studies Division | International Centre for Diarrhoeal Disease Research, Bangladesh) | Dhaka, Bangladesh | (February 2022 - June 2022)
Data Enumerator | School of Allied Health | Curtin University | Australia | (October 2022 ‑ November 2022)
Team Member | Bio-Bio-1:: Bioinformatics Research Discussion Group) | Dhaka, Bangladesh | (February 2017 - June 2019)


Programming Languages: Python, R, SQL, Julia, JavaScript; Data Science: scikit-learn, PyCaret, Dask, PySpark; GIS & Remote Sensing: ArcGIS, Geopandas, Xarray, Giovani, Google Earth Engiine; Analytics Softwares: SPSS, PowerBI, Microsoft Excel; Survey Tools: RedCap, KoboToolBox, EpiCollect, Google Forms; Academic Writing Tools: Microsoft Word, LaTeX, Mendeley; Bioinformatics: BioPython, Bioconductor, BioPandas, Galaxy, NGS, RNASeq, ssRNASeq; Miscellaneous Skills: UNIX, Version Control(Git), Web Scraping, APIs


Master of Science in Microbiology | Jagannath University, Dhaka (2020 - 2021)

Conducted an in‑depth project as part of my master’s program, centered on investigating and analyzing a Chikungunya outbreak, showcasing a dedicated commitment to advancing knowledge in the field of public health.

Bachelor of Science in Microbiology | Jagannath University, Dhaka (2016 - 2019)

Participated in a cutting‑edge project focused on the application of bioinformatics techniques for profiling antibiotic resistance genes within complex metagenomic DNA sequences.

AI in Public Health | Child Health Research Foundation (CHRF), Dhaka, Bangladesh (September 2023)

Participated in a comprehensive 4‑day workshop focused on utilizing AI techniques in public health research and decision‑making
Recipient of the scholarship, which provided financial support for my enrollment in the ’AI In Public Health’ workshop, as part of the Democra‑ tizing Public Health Modeling Using AI‑based Tools grant by the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation made to CHRF
